title: Zillow Group Reports Second-Quarter 2024 Financial Results
text: Zillow Q2 2024 Financial Results and Performance Analysis. Q2 2024 Results: Zillow reported Q2 revenue of $572 million, up 13% year over year. This result exceeded the company's conservative outlook guidance. Revenue Breakdown: Residential Revenue: $409 million (up 8% YoY). Rentals Revenue: $120 million (up 20% YoY). Mortgages Revenue: $36 million (down 10% YoY from Q2 2023). Profitability: GAAP net loss of $17 million for Q2 2024. Adjusted EBITDA of $102 million, representing an 18% margin. Key Metrics: Monthly unique users: 221 million (down 1% YoY). Total visits: 2.4 billion (up 1% YoY). Mortgage loan originations: $601 million in purchase volume. Strategic Progress: CEO Jeremy Wacksman noted the company's continued transformation into an AI-powered real estate platform. The first-quarter shareholder letter emphasized AI-driven productivity gains across the platform. Business Outlook: The company guided Q3 adjusted EBITDA of $135-150M, indicating confidence in near-term momentum despite the conservative initial Q2 forecast. Market Position: Zillow maintained its position as the leading real estate portal with strong traffic and continued expansion of high-margin businesses.
